Interface Validation<E,T>

Type Parameters:
E - the error element/container type
T - the validated result type
All Known Implementing Classes:
Invalid, Valid
public sealed interface Validation<E,T> permits Valid<E,T>, Invalid<E,T>
A disjoint union (sum type) representing a validated value Valid<T> or an accumulated List<E> of errors contained by Invalid<E>. This is NOT a "pair type". Validation is a special case XOR type for reasoning about accumulation of distinct errors.

Validation is:

  • right biased: operations like flatMap(CheckedFunction) target the Valid<T> validated value.
  • A monad: flatMap(CheckedFunction) chains dependent validations, short-circuiting on the first Invalid. For sequential error handling with short-circuiting use Either.
  • A bi-functor: bimap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction) transforms both Valid and Invalid results independently.
  • An applicative functor: combine(Validation, BiFunction) evaluates multiple independent validations and accumulates all errors, unlike flatMap(CheckedFunction) which short-circuits.
API Note:
Use Validation when you want to collect all validation errors (e.g. form validation) rather than stopping at the first failure. For sequential, non-accumulative error handling with short-circuiting use Either.
